title: quercus sororia
intro: Quercus sororia, also known as the white oak, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive white bark. The medicinal properties of Quercus sororia include its high tannin content, which has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Quercus sororia include its potential to reduce symptoms of arthritis, improve digestion, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus sororia can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to support liver and kidney health, and to treat conditions such as fever and sore throats.
